output:
Looking at this, I need to calculate the overall probability of arriving on time using the law of total probability.

**Given:**
- P(wake up late) = 5%, so P(wake up on time) = 95%
- P(arrive on time | wake up on time) = 52%
- P(arrive on time | wake up late) = 87%

**Calculation:**

P(arrive on time) = P(on time | wake on time) × P(wake on time) + P(on time | wake late) × P(wake late)

= 0.52 × 0.95 + 0.87 × 0.05
= 0.494 + 0.0435
= **0.5375 (53.75%)**

**Yes**, arriving to school on time (53.75%) is more likely than arriving late (46.25%).

An interesting detail: waking up late actually *increases* the chance of arriving on time (87% vs 52%) — perhaps the urgency motivates faster preparation. But since waking up late is rare (5%), the overall probability is dominated by the on-time wake-up scenario.
